name: Tim Holbert, American Veterans Center
subject: America’s culture is shifting
body:
Our schools used to teach every child about D-Day. Among the thousands of heroes of the longest day were the 12 soldiers who received the Medal of Honor for their courage and valor... but that’s no longer the case. WAs our Greatest Generation has passed, we have failed to teach our children the legacy of service, honor, and sacrifice of the men who fought their way into Hitler’s Fortress Europe.| | | [Unsubscribe](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_s=5eb7d4ec8a484457aca46220afe13790&_t=t6Y_EMzuc1NJrspiSRBcKcxjQUMKooxyioZ77q5cebVld01wUXVlHrG3tU4iAZUEphP5uQSAbqrZ4jtUwe4ZIIxhWSpXM-l4nx_KDXN6Kcg%3D) | [Mark as Junk](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_s=5eb7d4ec8a484457aca46220afe13790&_t=t6Y_EMzuc1NJrspiSRBcKcxjQUMKooxyioZ77q5cebVld01wUXVlHrG3tU4iAZUEphP5uQSAbqrZ4jtUwe4ZIIxhWSpXM-l4nx_KDXN6Kcg%3D)

[](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=5eb7d4ec8a484457aca46220afe13790&_e=2-6d-mjQlXJhNERUFaaJTuNfCfm0w4E0mGA1MggYuWsIKoCseZqq_NhkH_Fwv564XMd1u6T65rkJ-yL0X3ABoNlgMY1KgEQVh6kJjMHLEa_P3GrnFoEf7nmKB104e-7AVwg0D3rtOWrJiNavYiLYpCH3Ipz8kRQ5aJ8qrvxt9o7NgXMZwhsc2USkj6jP6_oGWr7KvaJ4OlLhu72_Y0bU6BrdzhHgRvQhqMsZYyQpqXesooZoiHaJ5LnMV8sy1RJa1ekzoiynXbsRoP-AQPCVBxWF9CeBBlKsVssJbfOhEaA-l-NkNHwiwff6xNMNdVDs)
---
Fellow Conservative, Our schools used to teach every child about D-Day. Among the thousands of heroes of the longest day were the 12 soldiers who received the Medal of Honor for their courage and valor... but that’s no longer the case. WAs our Greatest Generation has passed, we have failed to teach our children the legacy of service, honor, and sacrifice of the men who fought their way into Hitler’s Fortress Europe. In a recent poll of High School Seniors:
* 72% Didn’t know we fought Hitler during World War II.
* 87% Didn’t know we entered WWII because of the attacks on Pearl Harbor.
* 60% Didn’t know which countries we fought in WWII
* 13% Thought General Dwight Eisenhower was a general from the Civil War

Fellow Conservative, American kids are not being taught our nation’s history. According to ‘The Nation’s Report Card,’ America’s high schoolers’ proficiency in U.S. History and Civics is at its lowest level in nearly 30 years. They know little of the immense courage our troops displayed fighting for their freedoms in the Second World War, and they know little of the heroes who gave their life in service to our country. In fact, most Americans these days know next to nothing about World War II and the millions of young soldiers who put their lives on the line to serve their country in the war, let alone in Korea or Vietnam. To help educate young Americans about the selfless, patriotic, and courageous heroes in WWII, the American Veterans Center created a limited-edition booklet — **_A Mighty Endeavor: Operation Overlord through the Eyes of D-Day’s Greatest Heroes._** [**For the next 24 hours, I’m holding a copy of this brand new, one-of-a-kind booklet for you, Fellow Conservative. With your gift of any amount today, a copy of _A Mighty Endeavor_ will be yours to share with family and friends. **](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=5eb7d4ec8a484457aca46220afe13790&_e=2-6d-mjQlXJhNERUFaaJTuNfCfm0w4E0mGA1MggYuWsIKoCseZqq_NhkH_Fwv564XMd1u6T65rkJ-yL0X3ABoNlgMY1KgEQVh6kJjMHLEa_P3GrnFoEf7nmKB104e-7AVwg0D3rtOWrJiNavYiLYpCH3Ipz8kRQ5aJ8qrvxt9o6CIU23XUib5uNWOZ6ZSOCvGaXwr7djc2aWyerpsLKrHghwq4CkXuCJEOnMXmb7hp61qpjuJEC7PM3U0v_MY_zELzMMlRZp8UAtEkw4Ekw_iUXzitxn_-XX3gD1CzrEpDj4u7cMJheXkpFUYYwS3ClM)
---
[Claim your copy of **_A Mighty Endeavor_**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=5eb7d4ec8a484457aca46220afe13790&_e=2-6d-mjQlXJhNERUFaaJTuNfCfm0w4E0mGA1MggYuWsIKoCseZqq_NhkH_Fwv564XMd1u6T65rkJ-yL0X3ABoNlgMY1KgEQVh6kJjMHLEa_P3GrnFoEf7nmKB104e-7AVwg0D3rtOWrJiNavYiLYpCH3Ipz8kRQ5aJ8qrvxt9o43m5pYO6NGrbTwkhcIcz8oJbdzVmLaNyJ3x108_F6GdQ8fQBcz35zZ3LjS_Qz2Q5zQnC5zm2IviGpCrCYq_T0aWZeVlkMTGc4W1o0HkY2ywUaJB3JRUqxHA4R6PhCM_v2dp3dXs5EfyK0vp_zbzxc6)
---
[](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=5eb7d4ec8a484457aca46220afe13790&_e=2-6d-mjQlXJhNERUFaaJTuNfCfm0w4E0mGA1MggYuWsIKoCseZqq_NhkH_Fwv564XMd1u6T65rkJ-yL0X3ABoNlgMY1KgEQVh6kJjMHLEa_P3GrnFoEf7nmKB104e-7AVwg0D3rtOWrJiNavYiLYpCH3Ipz8kRQ5aJ8qrvxt9o6i_QQQgEW9MmJ0W04sYfMjXvA_fTlBXUBdJxNSYmgoBOK35cKOjZ7wmo1vjW58PQbEcrPjiKFPli1SjZcszdhwTByEjNf3srTtXAJdimDKrTMCp_qAS6s3QWASyeYOVkfVeLgPJK-xqJhmdOiechMs)
---
Every one of the 156,000 men who stormed the beaches of Normandy on that bloodyday is a hero in my book. It took real guts to run head-first into a hailstorm of bullets and mortar rounds. We all hope nobody ever needs to experience something like it again. But it is essential we remember, and learn. The AVC is devoted to preserving their history, so that today’s young Americans – and those in generations to come – can learn from the heroes you and I grew up idolizing. The 12 heroes who received the Medal of Honor showed so much courage and valor in the invasion of Normandy, that they were individually chosen to receive our nation’s highest honor. All Americans should read the stories and know of these heroes who have been all but forgotten. The [**American Veterans Center is committed to bringing the legacy of the greatest generation to the latest generation, and with your gift today, we can preserve the legacy of America’s World War II veterans.**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=5eb7d4ec8a484457aca46220afe13790&_e=2-6d-mjQlXJhNERUFaaJTuNfCfm0w4E0mGA1MggYuWsIKoCseZqq_NhkH_Fwv564XMd1u6T65rkJ-yL0X3ABoNlgMY1KgEQVh6kJjMHLEa_P3GrnFoEf7nmKB104e-7AVwg0D3rtOWrJiNavYiLYpCH3Ipz8kRQ5aJ8qrvxt9o5BqZdtCLz-_gzWU3-relCtsJaJLqP9e98_i-QBtocJHfIzHnN2cjZTl_bCe9PhDb3U-upSciDG1FDq88Yng9_k0OvY07lV76V0Uhpy-xdzX-u0Kb5Zyd31XxTNcouArr26PINfKBztgfgBDxXFoNSd) As a token of my thanks for your support, I’ll send you a limited-edition copy of **_A Mighty Endeavor_**. When you receive it, I have just one request; please share it with a loved one. Show them these stories and talk about how important their legacy is to our history. But, please let me know in the next 24 hours if you’re claiming your copy! It is my hope that you’ll share this booklet with your children, grandchildren, and neighbors and tell them all about D-Day.
---
[Claim your copy of **_A Mighty Endeavor_**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=5eb7d4ec8a484457aca46220afe13790&_e=2-6d-mjQlXJhNERUFaaJTuNfCfm0w4E0mGA1MggYuWsIKoCseZqq_NhkH_Fwv564XMd1u6T65rkJ-yL0X3ABoNlgMY1KgEQVh6kJjMHLEa_P3GrnFoEf7nmKB104e-7AVwg0D3rtOWrJiNavYiLYpCH3Ipz8kRQ5aJ8qrvxt9o6QKi-L7ekkPpYjk4Ak3GCiaXOdsG4BrUX8N_qQL2h2aGe-ZKCwpS5FYvqVguDRdUKjsn8Ui_Oe-rqW6rTdxrvhSImetzmxVoYH50fhmJFoDj2AV1oAXdPhtK1jpGtbAp_SwCI0gDpJjRYa_KkMOwue)
---
Fellow Conservative, we must share the TRUTH about American WWII veterans with America’s youth – we cannot fail them any longer. We must educate our nation’s children with messages of the D-Day heroes who were up against a battle-hardened enemy, whose very actions preserved the freedoms we enjoy today. [**Please support American Veterans Center’s mission to fight the historical ignorance plaguing today’s culture and teach America’s young people what real service meant during WWII.**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=5eb7d4ec8a484457aca46220afe13790&_e=2-6d-mjQlXJhNERUFaaJTuNfCfm0w4E0mGA1MggYuWsIKoCseZqq_NhkH_Fwv564XMd1u6T65rkJ-yL0X3ABoNlgMY1KgEQVh6kJjMHLEa_P3GrnFoEf7nmKB104e-7AVwg0D3rtOWrJiNavYiLYpCH3Ipz8kRQ5aJ8qrvxt9o4gQ9FHFda2iqlnZEqwf0-QjEiSfwQidUvS6MeyH2PKEept_GRCnrp5UhkHv4hiJQaURwuVNl7heIs_gZj7Xl0si5ojW4SjodDw3Zza-N8xBCR089HxM26oap1h0-B7bbB8DGRpG6pd1BbJ4awmIuLk) Whether you can afford to donate $15, $35, $50, $100, $250, $500, $1,000, or any other amount — I’ll send along your copy of **_A Mighty Endeavor: Operation Overlord through the Eyes of D-Day’s Greatest Heroes_** as a “thank you” for your support of our bravest Americans. Thank you and God bless,
---
Tim HolbertAmerican Veterans Center
---
[**Support American Veterans Center**](http://links.ourdefenseofamerica.com/u/click?_t=aa3ef0db03454a26b650c9ca7e919386&_m=5eb7d4ec8a484457aca46220afe13790&_e=2-6d-mjQlXJhNERUFaaJTuNfCfm0w4E0mGA1MggYuWsIKoCseZqq_NhkH_Fwv564XMd1u6T65rkJ-yL0X3ABoNlgMY1KgEQVh6kJjMHLEa_P3GrnFoEf7nmKB104e-7AVwg0D3rtOWrJiNavYiLYpCH3Ipz8kRQ5aJ8qrvxt9o6g2nnJshmsslphS60RLBohEZddJQy-C6jcrJSwRaWxarlKgPlIExzS4-RtRbqD2li6vMfsMd6vLnH0W4zh_HT_Ffugkk90YAi9_wUll4AjX6Ld6-EKup0f_5NyuQjsMZj0ECD_aisrtXz_jwzLA_f0)
---
The mission of the American Veterans Center and the World War II Veterans Committee is to preserve and promote the legacy and experiences of America's veterans and active duty service personnel from World War II through today. A non-profit educational organization, the AVC and WWIIVC are funded solely through generous contributions from people like you. All contributions are tax-deductible. © American Veterans Center
1100 N. Glebe Road Suite 910, Arlington, VA 22201
---

name: Exclusive Letter for Top Democrats (VPP)
subject: SIGN: Thank Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
She's a HERO!
| **BREAKING: Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson’s Passionate Defense Sways Conservative Justices to Protect Voting Rights Act** Folks, thanks to Supreme Court Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson, the Supreme Court shot down Alabama’s attempt to heavily gerrymander the state and silence Black voters.

[**We’re hand-delivering an urgent thank you card to her office at 9 AM tomorrow – so we only have 108 more signature spots available! SIGN NOW! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/49926?t=6&akid=9320%2E878179%2EVhSijj) [ ](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/49926?t=7&akid=9320%2E878179%2EVhSijj) | [DONATE $3 TO SIGN THE CARD! »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/49926?t=8&akid=9320%2E878179%2EVhSijj)
---
TO: Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson
FROM: A proud Democrat
Subject: You're a hero! **_Dear Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson — THANK YOU._**

Your impassioned defense of voting rights convinced even the most conservative Justices to uphold the Voting Rights Act and stand up to Alabama’s blatant discrimination.

I know going up against Donald Trump’s far-right majority on the Court isn’t easy, so I applaud your outstanding resilience to defend our democracy.

**I couldn’t be happier that President Biden nominated you!**

[**So I’m signing this card to thank you for all your courage and hard work over the last year, and will chip in $3 to the Voter Protection Project to elect incredible Democrats who will save our right to vote from corrupt Republican gerrymandering! »**](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/49926?t=9&akid=9320%2E878179%2EVhSijj) [ ](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/49926?t=10&akid=9320%2E878179%2EVhSijj) | [DONATE $3 TO SIGN NOW! »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/49926?t=11&akid=9320%2E878179%2EVhSijj)
---
**_X A proud Democrat_**
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=9320%2E878179%2EVhSijj). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=9320%2E878179%2EVhSijj).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=9320%2E878179%2EVhSijj) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=12&akid=9320%2E878179%2EVhSijj). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=9320%2E878179%2EVhSijj)
---
[Donate Just One Time »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/124?t=5&akid=9320%2E878179%2EVhSijj)

subject: re: $6 million
body:
Republicans in Virginia are scheming to take full control of the state government this November – and they just raised nearly $6 million more to fund their efforts. | | |
---
| | Republicans in Virginia are scheming to take full control of the state government this November – **and they just raised nearly $6 million more to fund their efforts.** Governor Glenn Youngkin even announced a new effort to boost **Republican turnout in Virginia by pushing early voting and mail-in ballots ahead of this fall’s ultra-competitive elections.** **The GOP is building a war chest and laying the groundwork to elect right-wing candidates in Virginia and across the country. They are flooding state legislative races with huge sums of cash to pass their extreme agenda and attack our fundamental freedoms.** [So, will you chip in $7 to the DLCC and support Democratic candidates in Virginia and beyond? _Jirair Ratevosian_
\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Jirair Ratevosian <[info@jirairforca.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66861841/417812305/-972311762?amount=15&refcode=JRFC_20230721_EMDF_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYxNDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDU5MTgwOTAtMjkyOC1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=lUSgKdf32JhnMGIvM-N4npbd2mP1b2JlQDESAhwN2sM=)>

Subject: I’m getting married to the love of my life — but the Supreme Court made discrimination against LGBTQ+ people legal.
To: <[dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66861842/417812306/-972311762?amount=15&refcode=JRFC_20230721_EMDF_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYxNDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDU5MTgwOTAtMjkyOC1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=lUSgKdf32JhnMGIvM-N4npbd2mP1b2JlQDESAhwN2sM=)>
**Team, I am still enraged at the recent SCOTUS decision to make discrimination legal.**
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66861843/417812307/-972311762?amount=15&refcode=JRFC_20230721_EMDF_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYxNDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDU5MTgwOTAtMjkyOC1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=lUSgKdf32JhnMGIvM-N4npbd2mP1b2JlQDESAhwN2sM=)
For those who don’t know, I am engaged to be married this fall to the love of my life, Micheal.

**I should be writing my vows and counting down the days until my wedding — but instead, we’re calling our wedding vendors to ensure they will continue to help us celebrate our wedding day.**

This decision puts people in danger. Point blank, it allows bigotry to run rampant throughout our community, making LGBTQ+ people feel unsafe in their own neighborhoods.

Obergefell v Hodges should have been the stop point of LGBTQ+ discrimination. Instead, the Supreme Court legalized it.

Now our gay brothers and sisters are not free from discrimination from private business. Now the free speech of religious zealots is favored, empowered, and protected.

**It’s enough. We need representatives in Congress that will fight day and night to protect our equal rights — not stand by while they are eroded by our far-right Supreme Court.**

[Team, I’m asking you to join my campaign tonight and help me win this seat. Representation matters — and it matters that much more when discrimination is deemed legal by our governing bodies. Will you give just $15 today to my campaign? »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/66861844/417812308/-972311762?amount=15&refcode=JRFC_20230721_EMDF_E1_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYxNDUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMDU5MTgwOTAtMjkyOC1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlMWJhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=lUSgKdf32JhnMGIvM-N4npbd2mP1b2JlQDESAhwN2sM=)

clean_body: I wanted to make sure you saw this. It’s important to me. | Hello Peter! Emails are our best tool to keep in touch with supporters, but if you'd like to receive fewer emails, . If you no longer want to receive emails from us, please . = I sent you an email last week about the Supreme Court decision to make discrimination against LGBTQ+ people legal. I didn’t hear back from you, so I wanted to make sure you saw this. Right now, I should be celebrating my upcoming wedding to the love of my life – instead, I’m constantly worried about what this decision could mean for me and my family’s future. = | = I’ve included my email from last week below. I can’t thank you enough for taking the time to read this, friend. Jirair Ratevosian \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Jirair Ratevosian <=> Date: Fri, Jul 14, 2023 at 7:01 PM Subject: I’m getting married to the love of my life — but the Supreme Court made discrimination against LGBTQ+ people legal. To: <=> Team, I am still enraged at the recent SCOTUS decision to make discrimination legal. = For those who don’t know, I am engaged to be married this fall to the love of my life, Micheal. I should be writing my vows and counting down the days until my wedding — but instead, we’re calling our wedding vendors to ensure they will continue to help us celebrate our wedding day. This decision puts people in danger. Point blank, it allows bigotry to run rampant throughout our community, making LGBTQ+ people feel unsafe in their own neighborhoods. Obergefell v Hodges should have been the stop point of LGBTQ+ discrimination. Instead, the Supreme Court legalized it. Now our gay brothers and sisters are not free from discrimination from private business. Now the free speech of religious zealots is favored, empowered, and protected. It’s enough. We need representatives in Congress that will fight day and night to protect our equal rights — not stand by while they are eroded by our far-right Supreme Court. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = = = = = = In solidarity, Jirair | | Paid for by Jirair Ratevosian For Congress 321 N. name: =?utf-8?Q?Illinois=20Review?=
subject: Pekau's Peril: If He Builds It, Will They Come?
body:
Hoping to jumpstart his political career and the local economy, Pekau embarks on building his "Field of Dreams." Only this isn't Iowa Baseball.

View this email in your browser (https://mailchi.mp/b354f3686377/pekaus-peril-if-he-builds-it-will-they-come?e=1cb5fdae9e)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=abd8a59afa-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_07_21_09_59&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-abd8a59afa-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D
Illinois Review eBlast Powered By
https://kasparlaw.co/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=abd8a59afa-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_07_21_09_59&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-abd8a59afa-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

Mayor Keith Pekau Brags About Large Crowd Size of 2,500 During Orland Park's Free Concert Last Weekend, But Actual Photos Reveal Less Than 50 in Attendance (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/07/mayor-keith-pekau-brags-about-large-crowd-size-of-2500-during-orland-parks-free-concert-last-weekend-but-actual-photos-reveal-less-than-50-in-attendance.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=abd8a59afa-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_07_21_09_59&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-abd8a59afa-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/07/mayor-keith-pekau-brags-about-large-crowd-size-of-2500-during-orland-parks-free-concert-last-weekend-but-actual-photos-reveal-less-than-50-in-attendance.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=abd8a59afa-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_07_21_09_59&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-abd8a59afa-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D
Less than 50 people attended the free concert in Orland Park last weekend, despite Mayor Keith Pekau claiming that were were "2,500 people" in attendance. (Source: Village of Orland Park/Facebook)

During Monday evening’s Village of Orland Park Board of Trustee meeting, Mayor Keith Pekau bragged about the attendance at last weekend’s free concert in the park – claiming there were “2,500 people at the last one,” as photos from concertgoers reveal that less than 50 were in attendance.
https://youtu.be/OeF8HZ9zMlE?si=nhnclcz86w4jLFWa&utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=abd8a59afa-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_07_21_09_59&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-abd8a59afa-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

But Pekau wasn’t the only one grossly exaggerating the number of people in attendance at the concert, as fellow trustee and close ally of the embattled mayor Sean Kampas reported back during the village trustee meeting that, “I know it was enjoyed because of the sheer number of people who came out.”
https://youtu.be/TCx-rWQKdp0?si=XTBrcgPbaqRSIqGq&utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=abd8a59afa-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_07_21_09_59&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-abd8a59afa-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

Kampas was made infamous when he joined Pekau – riding shotgun in his vehicle to harass (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/witnesses-keith-pekau-harasses-intimidates-female-orland-fire-dist-candidates-follows-them-in-his-car-while-they-knocked-on-doors.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=abd8a59afa-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_07_21_09_59&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-abd8a59afa-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) female Orland Fire Protection District candidates Angela Greenfield and Tina Zekich and their volunteers as they campaigned and knocked on doors in a local neighborhood just a week before the April elections.

It was reported by several witnesses that earlier in the day, the group of volunteers met at a local Starbucks – their usual meeting spot, to prepare for the day’s activities. But when they arrived, they were surprised to find Pekau and Kampas sitting at a nearby table, cups of coffee in hand, staring at the group and eavesdropping on their conversations – even accusing the off-duty firefighters of engaging in political activities, even though they were volunteering during personal time off.

When the group left, Pekau and Kampas followed them out of the coffee shop – trailing them in Pekau’s Dodge Durango as they headed to a nearby neighborhood to knock on doors and drop off literature.

Although Pekau and Kampas never got out of the car, numerous witnesses said the pair continued to follow the group – staring at them through the car windows in a sad attempt at intimidating the group of volunteers who were campaigning on behalf of candidates that they opposed.

And during community events throughout Orland Park, Kampas, an
IT consultant (https://www.linkedin.com/in/sean-kampas/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=abd8a59afa-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_07_21_09_59&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-abd8a59afa-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) , is often seen wearing an official Orland Park Police Emergency Services polo with an official police badge attached to his belt – giving the impression that he is a member of law enforcement.
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/07/mayor-keith-pekau-brags-about-large-crowd-size-of-2500-during-orland-parks-free-concert-last-weekend-but-actual-photos-reveal-less-than-50-in-attendance.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=abd8a59afa-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_07_21_09_59&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-abd8a59afa-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D
Eric Vates, left, of Orland Park Mazda, Sean Kampas, center, an IT consultant and Village Trustee, and Orland Park Mayor Keith Pekau pose for a photo during a community event last Summer. Vates and Kampas are wearing official Orland Park Police uniforms and a badge, even though they are not law enforcement. (Source: Village of Orland Park/Facebook)

In Illinois, it is unlawful to impersonate a police officer, and a Class 4 Felony (https://www.fenbertlaw.com/blog/2020/04/impersonating-a-police-officer-is-a-class-4-felony-in-illinois/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=abd8a59afa-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_07_21_09_59&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-abd8a59afa-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D#:~:text=If%20a%20person%20is%20caught,up%20to%20%2425%2C000%20in%20fines.) , punishable by up to three years in jail and $25,000 in fines.

Pekau has recently implemented a wildly unpopular new sales tax that surpasses Democratic-controlled Chicago’s sales tax, to help pay for a variety of his initiatives, including a
$10 million (https://patch.com/illinois/orlandpark/orland-imposes-new-utility-tax-raises-sales-tax?utm_term=article-slot-1&utm_source=newsletter-daily&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=newsletter&utm_content=email-test_E1:v1&user_email=a7d811a4a2701ec43d310edcd78a40c94541f75ebbc2b3298de7572df95983c0&utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=abd8a59afa-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_07_21_09_59&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-abd8a59afa-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) concert stage at Centennial Park West.

Reports suggest that the price tag for the concert stage is closer to $16 million – but Pekau continues to claim that it won’t cost any more than $10 million to $12 million to build.

However, if the attendance at free concerts is any indication of the lack of interest and support – the idea of building a multi-million dollar concert venue where residents will have to pay for shows – seems like a concept that will be dead on arrival and a total waste of taxpayer money.

The embattled mayor remains incredibly unpopular, and the last several events he’s hosted around town have drawn extremely small crowds.

In March, Pekau hosted a
town hall meeting (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/03/mayor-keith-pekau-attacks-insults-constituents-online-proving-hes-a-better-fit-as-a-server-at-ed-debevics-than-mayor-of-orland-park.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=abd8a59afa-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_07_21_09_59&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-abd8a59afa-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) to discuss his referendum to expand his power and influence on all village matters, with an estimated 15 people in attendance despite the room being set up for 100.
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/07/mayor-keith-pekau-brags-about-large-crowd-size-of-2500-during-orland-parks-free-concert-last-weekend-but-actual-photos-reveal-less-than-50-in-attendance.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=abd8a59afa-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_07_21_09_59&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-abd8a59afa-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D
Orland Park Mayor Keith Pekau, top right, walking to the front to address a nearly-empty room at the Orland Park Public Library on March 22, 2023. (Source: Illinois Review)

A few weeks later, Pekau would suffer humiliating defeats (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2023/04/orland-park-voters-reject-keith-pekaus-power-grab-delivering-the-mayor-humiliating-defeats-in-election-night-disaster.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=abd8a59afa-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_07_21_09_59&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-abd8a59afa-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) during the April election as residents resoundingly rejected his power grab dubbed as a “referendum” by a shocking 31 points.

But that wasn’t all. Voters also rejected Pekau’s slate of endorsed candidates, losing 9 out of 10 races – proving that an overwhelming majority of Orland Park residents rejected Pekau and anyone associated with the power-hungry and divisive mayor.

And while Pekau continues to attract incredibly small crowds – sources confirm to Illinois Review that the troubled mayor is still focused on launching another congressional campaign to challenge Democrat-incumbent US. Rep. Sean Casten.

Last November, Pekau would lose the General Election to Casten by 10 points.

name: Tom Fitton
subject: Election Integrity SUCCESS!
body:
Secret Service SECRETS!

[INSIDE JW]

Illinois Settles Lawsuit, Agrees to Make Voter Registration List
Information Public

[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=29890&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cueW91dHViZS5jb20vd2F0Y2g/dj13SjR1eFd2cnY5USZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=54f9be8759095b36a9504ef14cd65b015d8d8d111c612f255d595ee8e46560b8]

We continue our success in ensuring cleaner elections.

Judicial Watch settled
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=29887&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2lsbGlub2lzLWNvbnNlcnZhdGl2ZS11bmlvbi1ldC1hbC12LWlsbGlub2lzLWV0LWFsLXNldHRsZW1lbnQtMDU1NDIvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=30c902825390024ca6710d1dcb9ef333e6bd2cddd2e4d6a6bd308fec0b7b6226]
a federal election integrity lawsuit on behalf of the Illinois
Conservative Union against the state of Illinois, the Illinois State
Board of Elections, and its director. The settlement grants access to
the current centralized statewide list of registered voters in the
state for the past 15 elections.

This is a victory for all legal voters in Illinois. Voters will now
have the transparency that federal law requires in order to ensure
elections in Illinois are more honest and cleaner. Clean voter rolls
mean cleaner elections.

State officials had refused to allow the nonprofit and three lawfully
registered Illinois voters to obtain a copy of the state’s voter
registration list, despite their lawful request for those records
under federal law.

The State Board allowed access to the records but made any meaningful
review impossible, requiring the plaintiffs to travel to Springfield,
Illinois during limited working hours and review Illinois’ millions
of voter records one at a time on a computer terminal, with no ability
to sort or organize records.

We filed the lawsuit
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=20594&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcHJlc3MtcmVsZWFzZXMvaWxsaW5vaXMtcmVmdXNlcy12b3Rlci1yb2xsLWRhdGEvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=cf9daff12e2334fd96987b197216f010b7e16dac94339489044bc5c2dee8e2aa]
in
the United States District Court in the Northern District of Illinois
(_Illinois Conservative Union et al v. Illinois et al__._
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=20577&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2lsbGlub2lzLWNvbnNlcnZhdGl2ZS11bmlvbi1ldC1hbC12LWlsbGlub2lzLWV0LWFsLWNvbXBsYWludC0wNTU0Mi8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=f4531b8391a72f440c90f0473038938309f05ccb0392796d33d82674460fd8f2]
(No.
1:20-cv-05542)).

The settlement was approved by U.S. District Judge Sara L. Ellis, and
states:

> Pursuant to this Agreement, Defendants shall provide to Plaintiffs
> the current centralized statewide list of registered voters for
> Illinois (the “Illinois Voter Registration List”) in electronic
> format, with all fields provided to political committees, including
> but not limited to fields indicating the registrant’s full name
> ... residential street address ... email address ... telephone
> number, county and state voter identification number, age of the
> registrant, and the registrant’s status (active or inactive) and
> the most recent date the entry was changed, and voting history for
> the last fifteen (15) elections.
The National Voter Registration Act of 1993
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=435&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVzdGljZS5nb3YvY3J0L25hdGlvbmFsLXZvdGVyLXJlZ2lzdHJhdGlvbi1hY3QtMTk5My1udnJhP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=f158da4a5ae1de35e565a6a8f53da89cd8322253d93d918bf210c72ca3be414b]
(NVRA)
provides that states “shall make available for public inspection
and, where available, photocopying at a reasonable cost, all records
concerning the implementation of programs and activities conducted for
the purpose of ensuring the accuracy and currency of official lists of
eligible voters.”

When members of the Illinois Conservative Union sought access to
Illinois’ voter list, however, they were told they must view the
database one record at a time, on a single computer screen, during
“normal business hours,” at the State Board of Elections office in
Springfield, Illinois, which is 200 miles from where they live. There
are over 8 million voter registrations in Illinois. Judicial Watch
argued that Illinois’ arbitrary restrictions “make a mockery” of
federal law, “as much as a requirement that Plaintiffs wear
blindfolds.”

In 2021, Judge Ellis ruled on a motion
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=27399&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY291cnQtcnVsZXMtdm90ZXItcm9sbHMtaWxsaW5vaXMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=ca0e41fd5f6d22545029ec97dd2711e5c0c846f6304a7c85ee2392dfbd21088c]
in
the case that, “Plaintiffs have plausibly alleged that” Illinois
law “conflicts with” and “and frustrates the NVRA’s purpose of
providing voter information to the public to help ensure the accuracy
and currency of voter registration rolls.” She also allowed a claim
to proceed under the Equal Protection Clause of the Fourteenth
Amendment, on the ground that political committees in Illinois can
access copies of the voter registration database while ordinary
citizens cannot. The claims were made against Illinois’ chief state
elections official, Bernadette Matthews, the Acting Executive Director
of the Illinois State Board of Elections.

A forthcoming Judicial Watch study, based on recent census data and
information Illinois reported to the federal Election Assistance
Commission, reveals that 14% of Illinois’ counties have more
registered voters than citizens over 18, while Illinois as a whole has
close to 800,000 inactive registrants.

As several federal courts have recognized, the public records
provisions of the National Voter Registration Act were intended to
enhance the ability of private groups to monitor whether states are
removing ineligible voters from their voter rolls. In April 2020, a
federal court in Maryland
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=18613&F=T&&hash=f9cd3d9e6cad361e01200d53e08c9a877feb8785547f446508071d6bbef12f01]
noted
that organizations “such as Judicial Watch” have “the resources
and expertise that few individuals can marshal. By excluding these
organizations from access to voter registration lists,” the purpose
of the federal law is undermined. That court ordered Maryland to
produce complete voter registration records requested by Judicial
Watch.

As you know, we are a national leader in voting integrity and voting
rights. As part of its work, Judicial Watch assembled a team of highly
experienced voting rights attorneys who stopped discriminatory
elections in Hawaii, and cleaned up voter rolls in California, Ohio,
Indiana, and Kentucky, among other achievements
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=21208&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvdGFnL252cmEvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=6895639061ab0e9dd273a0f81e79d0c7c0d5d5fea1149f126ef741e00ac8c21d].

Robert Popper, a Judicial Watch senior attorney, leads its election
law program. Popper was previously in the Voting Section of the Civil
Rights Division of the Justice Department, where he managed voting
rights investigations, litigations, consent decrees, and settlements
in dozens of states.

In April 2023, Pennsylvania settled
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=29522&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcGVubnN5bHZhbmlhLXNldHRsZXMtdm90ZXItcm9sbC1sYXdzdWl0Lz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=b33f9b5e5f1a5e111c3a1560505b53cf54fa81004f211534520c105700c0a578]

In March 2023, Colorado agreed
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=29191&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvY29sb3JhZG8tdG8tc2V0dGxlLWxhd3N1aXQtb3Zlci1pbmVsaWdpYmxlLXZvdGVycy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=f7260ef63b15ec87f6d3c20cddc85a268ea7c93313b47b76439fc1861bc9d82c]
to
settle our NVRA lawsuit alleging that Colorado failed to remove

we
filed in 2017.

We settled
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=28646&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvanctYW5kLW55Yy1zZXR0bGUtbGF3c3VpdC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=f85eb002f4b9dfa8334e25686194139c3301a613cd8d8d197cbaf3272fc1aa08]

BREAKING: JUDICIAL WATCH SUES FOR RECORDS ON SECRET SERVICE PROTECTION
FOR UNACKNOWLEDGED GRANDDAUGHTER OF JOE BIDEN

The family of the president of the United States obviously has special
security needs, provided first and foremost by the Secret Service. So,
when a Biden family member evidently has no protection, it is an issue
of public concern. Judicial Watch filed a Freedom of Information Act
(FOIA) lawsuit against the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) for
all internal Secret Service communications regarding the provision or
potential provision of Secret Service protection for Navy Joan
Roberts, the 4-year-old daughter of Hunter Biden and grandchild of
President Joe Biden (_Judicial Watch v. U.S. Department of Homeland
Security_
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=29880&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9jdW1lbnRzL2p3LXYtZGhzLW5hdnktam9hbi1yb2JlcnRzLWNvbXBsYWludC0wMjAyOC8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=6fbcb73620eed9cae2a82a1a9377596d2470473aa32f76ef231b42a53c5a3ff3]
(No.
1:23-cv-02028)).

On its website
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=29881&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuc2VjcmV0c2VydmljZS5nb3YvcHJvdGVjdGlvbi9sZWFkZXJzP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=f9fe7d15f1b3a84ed0d472402e2ef0af71d29383541002c5496f2334b4fcf248],
the Secret Service states that, under Title 18 of the United States
Code, Section 3056, the agency’s authority as provided by law
guarantees protection for the president and vice president and their
immediate families.

However, on June 6, 2022, the _Daily Mail_ reported
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=29882&F=T&&hash=256dbc44ec2c827b2b6eb7352287aee1d8775609cce52684524a781e206834e9]
that
President Biden refused to provide security for Navy Joan after her
mother, Lunden Roberts, reported to police that she had been
threatened by her ex-fiancé, cage fighter Princeton Foster.

We filed a FOIA request on May 2, 2023. On June 12, 2023, the Secret
Service issued a final response stating that no responsive records had
been located. We appealed, challenging the thoroughness of the search.
We then sued after the Secret Service failed to respond to the appeal
for:

> All reports, memoranda, and internal USSS electronic communications,
> including emails and text messages, regarding the provision or
> potential provision of USSS protection to Navy Joan Roberts, the
> daughter of Hunter Biden and granddaughter of President Joe Biden.
Hunter Biden had denied paternity, but a DNA test
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=29883&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYXJrYW5zYXNvbmxpbmUuY29tL25ld3MvMjAxOS9ub3YvMjAvZG5hLXRlc3Qtc2hvd3MtaHVudGVyLWJpZGVuLWZhdGhlci1hcmthbnNhcy13b21hbnMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPXdlZWtseSt1cGRhdGUmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycw==&hash=7c63abda141d44b21af7306d340976e0c8f81411965f8640f271d5e41e50e52e]
in
2019 proved he is in fact the father of Navy Joan Roberts and he has
been providing significant court-ordered child support.

President Biden has never publicly acknowledged that Navy Joan Roberts
is his grandchild. _The_ _New York Times_ reported
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=29884&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubnl0aW1lcy5jb20vMjAyMy8wNy8wMS91cy9wb2xpdGljcy9odW50ZXItYmlkZW4tZGF1Z2h0ZXItYXJrYW5zYXMuaHRtbD91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=b3f7af2785c65b5e73ab204d22b7a2e7a4d930d4bc09f02d99eb242b82351e2b]
earlier
this month: “In strategy meetings in recent years, aides have been
told that the Bidens have six, not seven, grandchildren, according to
two people familiar with the discussions...the president has not yet
met or publicly mentioned his other grandchild. His White House has
not answered questions about whether he will publicly acknowledge her
now that the child support case is settled.”

All of Joe Biden’s grandchildren can receive Secret Service
protection while he’s in office. We frankly can’t imagine any good
reason to deny security to this child.

MILLIONS MORE TO RECRUIT MINORITIES FOR JOBLESS BENEFITS AMID LOW
UNEMPLOYMENT

Bureaucracies never give up when it comes to spending your tax
dollars. As our _Corruption Chronicles _blog reports
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=29878&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcmVjcnVpdC1taW5vcml0aWVzLz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=e32c2bca9b41f1985fba55b7bec9c6b0f0707544db70e8ab067e935d9e720209],
President
Biden’s Labor Department is on another crusade to spend more tax
dollars:

> U.S. unemployment is low and stable, yet the Biden administration
> keeps giving states millions of dollars to boost the number of
> minorities—especially non-English speaking or limited-English
> proficient—that sign up for government jobless benefits. The goal
> is to address racial disparities and promote “equitable access”
> to the program. The money—an unprecedented $260 million
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=29891&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvMjYwLW1pbC10by1wcm9tb3RlLWVxdWl0eS9d4oCUc3RhcnRlZD91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=eac435f52749e9e0330d5ab4d2316e37139c5465ee28201c4223edb2ee164804
> flowing in early spring of 2022 to advance equity in state
> unemployment insurance. COVID-19 inspired the controversial project,
> according to the Department of Labor (DOL), the agency disbursing
> the funds. “Throughout the pandemic, disparities in access to
> benefits affected women, communities of color and other marginalized
> workers at a higher rate and often delayed delivery of much needed
> financial support and services,” the DOL announced last spring.
> “These disparities in access to unemployment insurance exposed
> serious real-world shortcomings in the outdated systems used to
> deliver state and territories unemployment insurance benefits.”
>
> Specifically, the Biden administration aims to address disparities
> in the administration and delivery of unemployment benefits by race,
> ethnicity, language proficiency and disability status. To accomplish
> it, the DOL has directed states to use the taxpayer dollars to
> “support innovative strategies and solutions to promote equitable
> access” to unemployment compensation. “This includes funding
> that will increase public awareness of the program so more people
> apply, improve service delivery so claimants receive their first
> benefits in a timely manner and develop a better understanding of
> the equity challenges that need to be addressed,” the labor agency
> revealed last year. State officials can also use a portion of their
> grant to evaluate the effectiveness of programs they create with the
> federal funds. “To become a more robust safety net and economic
> stabilizer, our unemployment insurance system must serve all workers
> fairly and equitably,” Labor Secretary Marty Walsh declared.
>
> The initial allocation of $20 million was split between three
> states—Oregon, Pennsylvania and Virginia—and the District of
> Columbia. Since then, the DOL has approved equity grants for 41
> states and jurisdictions, a recent agency filing
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=29892&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly9vdWkuZG9sZXRhLmdvdi91bmVtcGxveS9wZGYvQUlSX0FSUEFfU3RhdGVFcXVpdHlHcmFudHNTdW1tYXJ5TWVtb19GaW5hbC5wZGZdcmV2ZWFscz91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=b0c705d06b9261e93e5995ad269d90e5d10f422befaddeb09c55810065711d26.
> The document says that projects target a “wide range of
> underserved populations,” but identifies the top three as
> non-English or limited-English proficient, people with disabilities
> and low-income claimants with economic hardships. “States are
> continuing to use a diverse slate of strategies to promote equitable
> access to unemployment compensation programs,” the DOL writes.
> “Improving services to claimants and expanding access to services
> by improving technology or implementing new technology are primary
> areas of focus for most projects, with a core theme of resolving
> access barriers for underserved populations.” Some states are
> creating “customer outreach plans” that incorporate
> community-based groups representing the underserved populations.
>
> Even though unemployment remains low, a few days ago the
> administration allocated an additional $9.5 million
>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=29893&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZG9sLmdvdi9uZXdzcm9vbS9yZWxlYXNlcy9ldGEvZXRhMjAyMzA3MTMtMD91dG1fc291cmNlPWRlcGxveWVyJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWwmdXRtX2NvbnRlbnQ9QU1qY3dPRGs1TlRRJTNEJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj13ZWVrbHkrdXBkYXRlJnV0bV90ZXJtPW1lbWJlcnM=&hash=666c6fc0b4aedaf1dbaf7ad48e34b3a221fa493a5f3367140efdba0f88f1324b]
to sign up
> more minorities for unemployment benefits. The latest equity grants
> will go to Arkansas, Louisiana and Vermont so officials can
> implement projects that seek to remove barriers related to race,
> age, ethnicity, language proficiency and other system issues that
> make it difficult for people to access unemployment insurance
> benefits. Arkansas will get the biggest chunk, $4,562,000, with
> Louisiana receiving $2,661,616 and Vermont $2,283,000 to expand
> outreach, promote awareness, provide translation services and
> address disadvantaged communities’ other needs, according to the

>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=29894&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuY29tbWVyY2UuZ292L25ld3MvYmxvZy8yMDIzLzAyL25ld3MtdW5lbXBsb3ltZW50LWl0cy1sb3dlc3QtbGV2ZWwtNTQteWVhcnM/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249d2Vla2x5K3VwZGF0ZSZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=68a8f2a056accb7b699b75712f05507c3b7e9729bd6d7f76246be26f316ae029]
> about the record low unemployment rate among minorities,
> specifically black (5.4%) and Hispanic (4.5%) populations.
>
> The minority unemployment benefit drive is part of a larger Biden

>
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=64265&L=27254&F=T&&hash=09eea1ff59f712d6becdff4834520b1419637c8e799f25024e6f3ba157ae1a74]

clean_body: Secret Service SECRETS! [INSIDE JW] Illinois Settles Lawsuit, Agrees to Make Voter Registration List Information Public [] We continue our success in ensuring cleaner elections. Judicial Watch settled [] a federal election integrity lawsuit on behalf of the Illinois Conservative Union against the state of Illinois, the Illinois State Board of Elections, and its director. The settlement grants access to the current centralized statewide list of registered voters in the state for the past 15 elections. This is a victory for all legal voters in Illinois. Voters will now have the transparency that federal law requires in order to ensure elections in Illinois are more honest and cleaner. Clean voter rolls mean cleaner elections. State officials had refused to allow the nonprofit and three lawfully registered Illinois voters to obtain a copy of the state’s voter registration list, despite their lawful request for those records under federal law. The State Board allowed access to the records but made any meaningful review impossible, requiring the plaintiffs to travel to Springfield, Illinois during limited working hours and review Illinois’ millions of voter records one at a time on a computer terminal, with no ability to sort or organize records. We filed the lawsuit [] in the United States District Court in the Northern District of Illinois (Illinois Conservative Union et al v. Illinois et al. [] (No. 1:20-cv-05542)). The settlement was approved by U.S. District Judge Sara L. Ellis, and states: Pursuant to this Agreement, Defendants shall provide to Plaintiffs the current centralized statewide list of registered voters for Illinois (the “Illinois Voter Registration List”) in electronic format, with all fields provided to political committees, including but not limited to fields indicating the registrant’s full name ... residential street address ... email address ... telephone number, county and state voter identification number, age of the registrant, and the registrant’s status (active or inactive) and the most recent date the entry was changed, and voting history for the last fifteen (15) elections. The National Voter Registration Act of 1993 [] (NVRA) provides that states “shall make available for public inspection and, where available, photocopying at a reasonable cost, all records concerning the implementation of programs and activities conducted for the purpose of ensuring the accuracy and currency of official lists of eligible voters.” When members of the Illinois Conservative Union sought access to Illinois’ voter list, however, they were told they must view the database one record at a time, on a single computer screen, during “normal business hours,” at the State Board of Elections office in Springfield, Illinois, which is 200 miles from where they live. There are over 8 million voter registrations in Illinois. Judicial Watch argued that Illinois’ arbitrary restrictions “make a mockery” of federal law, “as much as a requirement that Plaintiffs wear blindfolds.” In 2021, Judge Ellis ruled on a motion [] in the case that, “Plaintiffs have plausibly alleged that” Illinois law “conflicts with” and “and frustrates the NVRA’s purpose of providing voter information to the public to help ensure the accuracy and currency of voter registration rolls.” She also allowed a claim to proceed under the Equal Protection Clause of the Fourteenth Amendment, on the ground that political committees in Illinois can access copies of the voter registration database while ordinary citizens cannot. The claims were made against Illinois’ chief state elections official, Bernadette Matthews, the Acting Executive Director of the Illinois State Board of Elections. A forthcoming Judicial Watch study, based on recent census data and information Illinois reported to the federal Election Assistance Commission, reveals that 14% of Illinois’ counties have more registered voters than citizens over 18, while Illinois as a whole has close to 800,000 inactive registrants. As several federal courts have recognized, the public records provisions of the National Voter Registration Act were intended to enhance the ability of private groups to monitor whether states are removing ineligible voters from their voter rolls. In April 2020, a federal court in Maryland [] noted that organizations “such as Judicial Watch” have “the resources and expertise that few individuals can marshal. By excluding these organizations from access to voter registration lists,” the purpose of the federal law is undermined. That court ordered Maryland to produce complete voter registration records requested by Judicial Watch. As you know, we are a national leader in voting integrity and voting rights. As part of its work, Judicial Watch assembled a team of highly experienced voting rights attorneys who stopped discriminatory elections in Hawaii, and cleaned up voter rolls in California, Ohio, Indiana, and Kentucky, among other achievements []. Robert Popper, a Judicial Watch senior attorney, leads its election law program. Popper was previously in the Voting Section of the Civil Rights Division of the Justice Department, where he managed voting rights investigations, litigations, consent decrees, and settlements in dozens of states. In April 2023, Pennsylvania settled [] with us and admitted in court filings that it removed 178,258 ineligible registrations in response to communications from Judicial Watch. The settlement commits Pennsylvania and five of its counties to extensive public reporting of statistics regarding their ongoing voter roll clean-up efforts for the next five years. In March 2023, Colorado agreed [] to settle our NVRA lawsuit alleging that Colorado failed to remove ineligible voters from its rolls. The settlement agreement requires Colorado to provide Judicial Watch with the most recent voter roll data for each Colorado county each year for six years. In February 2023, Los Angeles County confirmed [] the removal of 1,207,613 ineligible voters from its rolls since last year, under the terms of a settlement agreement [] in a federal lawsuit [] we filed in 2017. We settled [] a federal election integrity lawsuit against New York City after the city removed 441,083 ineligible names from the voter rolls and promised to take reasonable steps going forward to clean its voter registration lists. Kentucky [] also removed hundreds of thousands of old registrations after it entered into a consent decree to end another Judicial Watch lawsuit. In February 2022, we settled [] a voter roll clean-up lawsuit against North Carolina and two of its counties after North Carolina removed over 430,000 inactive registrations from its voter rolls. In March 2022, a Maryland court ruled in favor [] of our challenge to the Democratic state legislature’s “extreme” congressional gerrymander. In May 2022, we sued [] Illinois on behalf of Congressman Mike Bost and two other registered Illinois voters to stop state election officials from extending Election Day for 14 days beyond the date established by federal law. This litigation is ongoing. There is more work to be done and Judicial Watch will continue to do the heavy lifting to help ensure our elections are fair and honest. BREAKING: JUDICIAL WATCH SUES FOR RECORDS ON SECRET SERVICE PROTECTION FOR UNACKNOWLEDGED GRANDDAUGHTER OF JOE BIDEN The family of the president of the United States obviously has special security needs, provided first and foremost by the Secret Service. So, when a Biden family member evidently has no protection, it is an issue of public concern. Judicial Watch filed a Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) lawsuit against the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) for all internal Secret Service communications regarding the provision or potential provision of Secret Service protection for Navy Joan Roberts, the 4-year-old daughter of Hunter Biden and grandchild of President Joe Biden (Judicial Watch v. U.S. Department of Homeland Security [] (No. 1:23-cv-02028)). On its website [], the Secret Service states that, under Title 18 of the United States Code, Section 3056, the agency’s authority as provided by law guarantees protection for the president and vice president and their immediate families. However, on June 6, 2022, the Daily Mail reported [] that President Biden refused to provide security for Navy Joan after her mother, Lunden Roberts, reported to police that she had been threatened by her ex-fiancé, cage fighter Princeton Foster. We filed a FOIA request on May 2, 2023. On June 12, 2023, the Secret Service issued a final response stating that no responsive records had been located. We appealed, challenging the thoroughness of the search. We then sued after the Secret Service failed to respond to the appeal for: All reports, memoranda, and internal USSS electronic communications, including emails and text messages, regarding the provision or potential provision of USSS protection to Navy Joan Roberts, the daughter of Hunter Biden and granddaughter of President Joe Biden. Hunter Biden had denied paternity, but a DNA test [] in 2019 proved he is in fact the father of Navy Joan Roberts and he has been providing significant court-ordered child support. President Biden has never publicly acknowledged that Navy Joan Roberts is his grandchild. The New York Times reported [] earlier this month: “In strategy meetings in recent years, aides have been told that the Bidens have six, not seven, grandchildren, according to two people familiar with the discussions...the president has not yet met or publicly mentioned his other grandchild. His White House has not answered questions about whether he will publicly acknowledge her now that the child support case is settled.” All of Joe Biden’s grandchildren can receive Secret Service protection while he’s in office. We frankly can’t imagine any good reason to deny security to this child. MILLIONS MORE TO RECRUIT MINORITIES FOR JOBLESS BENEFITS AMID LOW UNEMPLOYMENT Bureaucracies never give up when it comes to spending your tax dollars. As our Corruption Chronicles blog reports [], President Biden’s Labor Department is on another crusade to spend more tax dollars: U.S. unemployment is low and stable, yet the Biden administration keeps giving states millions of dollars to boost the number of minorities—especially non-English speaking or limited-English proficient—that sign up for government jobless benefits. The goal is to address racial disparities and promote “equitable access” to the program. The money—an unprecedented $260 million [ flowing in early spring of 2022 to advance equity in state unemployment insurance. COVID-19 inspired the controversial project, according to the Department of Labor (DOL), the agency disbursing the funds. “Throughout the pandemic, disparities in access to benefits affected women, communities of color and other marginalized workers at a higher rate and often delayed delivery of much needed financial support and services,” the DOL announced last spring. “These disparities in access to unemployment insurance exposed serious real-world shortcomings in the outdated systems used to deliver state and territories unemployment insurance benefits.” Specifically, the Biden administration aims to address disparities in the administration and delivery of unemployment benefits by race, ethnicity, language proficiency and disability status. To accomplish it, the DOL has directed states to use the taxpayer dollars to “support innovative strategies and solutions to promote equitable access” to unemployment compensation. “This includes funding that will increase public awareness of the program so more people apply, improve service delivery so claimants receive their first benefits in a timely manner and develop a better understanding of the equity challenges that need to be addressed,” the labor agency revealed last year. State officials can also use a portion of their grant to evaluate the effectiveness of programs they create with the federal funds. “To become a more robust safety net and economic stabilizer, our unemployment insurance system must serve all workers fairly and equitably,” Labor Secretary Marty Walsh declared. The initial allocation of $20 million was split between three states—Oregon, Pennsylvania and Virginia—and the District of Columbia. Since then, the DOL has approved equity grants for 41 states and jurisdictions, a recent agency filing [. The document says that projects target a “wide range of underserved populations,” but identifies the top three as non-English or limited-English proficient, people with disabilities and low-income claimants with economic hardships. “States are continuing to use a diverse slate of strategies to promote equitable access to unemployment compensation programs,” the DOL writes. “Improving services to claimants and expanding access to services by improving technology or implementing new technology are primary areas of focus for most projects, with a core theme of resolving access barriers for underserved populations.” Some states are creating “customer outreach plans” that incorporate community-based groups representing the underserved populations. Even though unemployment remains low, a few days ago the administration allocated an additional $9.5 million [] to sign up more minorities for unemployment benefits. The latest equity grants will go to Arkansas, Louisiana and Vermont so officials can implement projects that seek to remove barriers related to race, age, ethnicity, language proficiency and other system issues that make it difficult for people to access unemployment insurance benefits. Arkansas will get the biggest chunk, $4,562,000, with Louisiana receiving $2,661,616 and Vermont $2,283,000 to expand outreach, promote awareness, provide translation services and address disadvantaged communities’ other needs, according to the DOL. Not that long ago, the administration bragged [] about the record low unemployment rate among minorities, specifically black (5.4%) and Hispanic (4.5%) populations. The minority unemployment benefit drive is part of a larger Biden administration effort to advance racial equity and support for underserved communities through the federal government. The costly initiative was launched back in January 2021 when the president issued an executive order [] claiming that “entrenched disparities” in laws, public policies, and private institutions have denied equal opportunity to individuals and communities and that the health and climate crises have exposed inequities while a “historic movement for justice has highlighted the unbearable human costs of systemic racism.” Following the order, Uncle Sam started cutting checks for the initiative and assigning special equity chiefs and commissions at agencies throughout the government. Until next week, [Contribute] [] [32x32x1] [] [32x32x2] [] [32x32x3] [] [32x32x3] [] Judicial Watch, Inc. 425 3rd St Sw Ste 800 Washington, DC 20024 202.646.5172 © 2017 - 2023, All Rights Reserved Manage Email Subscriptions [] |

name: Read/Act News
subject: How should we elect the president?
body:
| | Please see a special message from our sponsor.
---
| | | | [](http://trk.readactnews.)
---
| |
Dear Fellow American, I am enclosing a survey on a very urgent matter. It will only take a moment of your time, but the topic is vital to our nation’s future. A movement is growing, led by progressives—but supported by many well-meaning Americans—to change the way we elect our president. In effect, it seeks to do away with the Electoral College as devised by the Framers of our Constitution. That’s why Hillsdale College is conducting a **National Survey on Presidential Selection.** By completing this survey and returning it to me as soon as possible, you will help Hillsdale College more clearly understand the views of mainstream Americans concerning this issue—views we will make available to policymakers and opinion leaders. You can complete the **National Survey on Presidential Selection **by using the link below: **[https://lp.hillsdale.edu/electoral-college-survey](http://trk.readactnews.-a7K9SY0Z_MSr-GjPZ1A)**
## Do you think that most Americans understand how the Electoral College works and its role in preserving free and representative government?
[YES](http://trk.readactnews.)[NO](http://trk.readactnews.-JqjrjilOPIOA4qEdKw)For most of our nation’s history, the Electoral College, as devised by the Framers of our Constitution, was not controversial. Indeed, it was considered essential to preserving free and representative government. **But today, more than a dozen states have joined together in an attempt to undermine this constitutional bulwark of liberty.** This movement to undermine the Constitution has grown largely because of the failure of America’s schools to provide young people with a proper grounding in American civics—too many Americans simply don’t understand the importance of the Constitution, including the Electoral College, to liberty. Because of this, Hillsdale College has expanded its educational mission on behalf of liberty nationwide, directly teaching **millions** of Americans in all 50 states. These efforts, which aim to counter the Left’s domination over most of our schools, include:
* **Teaching** millions of citizens nationwide through free online courses, such as “Constitution 101” and “The Great American Story: A Land of Hope;”
* **Reaching** over six million households and businesses nationwide with _Imprimis_ , Hillsdale’s free digest of liberty;
* **Promoting** constitutional education through social media outlets popular with young people;
* **Sending** millions of pocket-size copies of America’s founding documents to citizens and K-12 schools across America; and
* **Restoring** excellence to K-12 education by helping establish and guiding classical K-12 schools nationwide and providing, free of charge, Hillsdale’s American classical K-12 curriculum (including American civics) to thousands of American educators and homeschoolers.

Your answers to the **National Survey on Presidential Selection** will help Hillsdale form the most effective strategy to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty—including the importance of the Electoral College. **And this is especially important in this critical time for America’s future.** So please share your views on this urgent topic by completing our **National Survey on Presidential Selection** and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few minutes of your time. **You can take the survey using this secure link I've provided below:

**[https://lp.hillsdale.edu/electoral-college-survey](http://trk.readactnews._3RExRPjdOUQO0l6eNQ)**
** And after you take your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift. Since 1844, Hillsdale has been “pursuing truth and defending liberty.” Today, it does so through its extensive educational outreach efforts while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans. The support of private citizens like you is essential to our work. Please represent your state and take the **National Survey on Presidential Selection today**. Thank you for your time and attention—and for your friendship and support of liberty. Warm regards, Larry P. ArnnPresident, Hillsdale CollegePursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844
---
Read/Act News PO Box 455 Leesburg, VA 20178If you no longer wish to receive sponsored promotional emails from us [_click here_](http://trk.readactnews.com/uu/3/eyJhaSI6OTkyNjA2OTcsImUiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsInJpIjoiOTgzNjUxNjA3IiwicnEiOiIwMi1iMjMyMDMtZjYxYTNlYmRhYjE4NGU4NGE2NTFlMjU4ODc1OTU5MmMiLCJwaCI6bnVsbCwibSI6ZmFsc2V9/3YBlEh9H_-JHoW3ISo0_5g) to unsubscribe.
---

name: CPT Sam Brown
subject: I’m sorry, but we can’t go on like this.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sam Brown for Nevada
body:
Americans can’t take another 6 years of this

| | |
---
Friend, _Our schools are failing parents and students._ _Our economy is tanking._ _Our money is worth less and everything costs more._ _Our southern border is an invasion zone._ _Our brave policemen are being overrun by violent crime in cities across the country._ _Our politicians forget about their constituents._ Americans can't take another 6 years of this. **[I cannot, I WILL NOT stand idly by.](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2za9e7g2umvg35r6vnte9ahfel49j157v35udfogg_rp22sh2s8i66p70c1h60o34chh2bs)** My name is Sam Brown. I'm an Army Captain, Purple Heart recipient, small business owner, and proud husband and father. _**[I'm running for U.S. Senate in the most competitive Senate race in the country.](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z7qvvog2uc12h3a0gejuha1pri5o46fkumuuaeco_rp22sh2s8i66p70c1h60o34chh2bs)**_ My entire life has been a series of missions. Missions that I've gladly taken in service to my God and my country. **[My next mission is one that calls me to serve my country in the U.S. Senate. Will you help me continue my mission?](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2z3a85e27e7tmu0kak5dbo2jpjdlu9lmutpj1do08_rp22sh2s8i66p70c1h60o34chh2bs)**
---
[**SEND IN SAM**](http://e.deroymurdock.com/rd/9z2zg9f28rtusmvujfbl6kudse2u030aid19ft91p20_rp22sh2s8i66p70c1h60o34chh2bs)
---
Democrats are willing to spend whatever it takes to defeat me, and I'm counting on patriots like you to make sure they don't succeed. Thank you in advance for your support! Duty First, CPT Sam Brown, U.S. Army (Ret.)

name: 1600 Daily

name: CAPT Hung Cao, USN (Ret)
subject: I’m not done fighting
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Hung Cao for Virginia
body:
This is the sound that echoes in my memories, reminding me of the fear and oppression that comes with living in a communist country.

| | | [](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c662284%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=4rL0OTLI1AXE8HNPfeAxNQ&sysid=1)
---
**Knock, Knock, Knock. **
---
This is the sound that echoes in my memories, reminding me of the fear and oppression that comes with living in a communist country.

My name is Hung Cao, and I am here to share my family's story with you. It is a story of fear, turned into hope and gratitude for the country that saved my life - America.

My parents heard dreadful knocking at their doors. It was the sound that took away their fathers, never to be seen again. It was the sound of living in constant fear, of losing one's freedom. They vowed their children would never witness that.

We escaped Vietnam just days before Saigon fell to the communists. America opened its arms to us, granting us a new life filled with opportunities.

I seized those opportunities, attending the renowned Thomas Jefferson High School for Science and Technology and graduating from the United States Naval Academy. I dedicated myself to service, spending 25 years in Navy Special Operations, fighting for our country in Iraq, Afghanistan, and Somalia.
---
[](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c662284%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=4rL0OTLI1AXE8HNPfeAxNQ&sysid=1)
---
But today, our country stands at a crossroads. We have taken a dark turn, where a knock at the door represents something entirely different...

It's the sound of Joe Biden's Justice Department sending armed agents to arrest a pro-life activist, tearing families apart before their very eyes. It's the sound of the IRS raiding gun shops, seizing the records of law-abiding gun owners - violating our privacy and trust. And shockingly, it's the sound of a former President being arrested, eroding the principles our nation was built upon.

Crime is running rampant, violating the safety of our homes and businesses, and destroying our communities. Criminals roam free while law-abiding citizens suffer. Our borders are flooded with illegal immigrants, and the dangerous influx of drugs like fentanyl threatens the lives of our loved ones.

In a democratic republic, the rules should apply to all, regardless of power or position. But the Biden family seems to operate above the law, reminding us of a dictatorship where rulers are exempt from accountability.

We cannot let our country slip away. The time for silence and forced compliance is over. We must stand up for what is right, for the truth that we are made to deny.

I have traveled the world and witnessed the consequences of failed nations. I promise you, if America falls, there is nowhere else to go. That's why I am stepping forward to fight for our shared values.
---
[](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c662284%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=4rL0OTLI1AXE8HNPfeAxNQ&sysid=1)
---
I believe in the American Dream, where equal opportunities are earned, not handed out. It's our duty to reclaim our dreams, protect our lives, and to safeguard the well-being of our families.

We need true fighters, not politicians and certainly not keyboard warriors. We need fearless individuals who understand the value of our freedoms and will fight tooth and nail to preserve them.

Together, we can make a difference. We can refuse to be intimidated, standing tall in the face of adversity. We can ensure that America remains the land of opportunity, where our children can thrive and our dreams can flourish.
---
[](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c662284%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=4rL0OTLI1AXE8HNPfeAxNQ&sysid=1)
---
**I am Hung Cao, retired Navy Captain, and I am running for the United States Senate. I am committed to being your voice, your champion, and your unwavering fighter.**

Join me in this fight for our country's future. Together, we can reclaim what we hold dear and ensure that America remains a beacon of hope and liberty for generations to come. **[https://secure.winred.com/hung-cao-for-virginia/im-running-for-senate](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c662295%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=WnV7u51YE%2bpFXn3rIP5uBA&sysid=1)**
---
I'm Hung Cao and I'm not done fighting for us.

Thank you for your support,
---
[](https://click.americanupdate.com/Pages/Click.aspx?ghff34ghj=01%7c662284%7c210334%7c805%7cqcjvyyvf12%40tznvy.pbz&wdmdk=4rL0OTLI1AXE8HNPfeAxNQ&sysid=1)
---
CAPT Hung Cao, USN (Ret) Former Navy Special Operations Candidate, U.S. Senate (VA)
---

The time for silence and forced compliance is over. We must stand up for what is right, for the truth that we are made to deny. I have traveled the world and witnessed the consequences of failed nations. I promise you, if America falls, there is nowhere else to go. That's why I am stepping forward to fight for our shared values. I believe in the American Dream, where equal opportunities are earned, not handed out. It's our duty to reclaim our dreams, protect our lives, and to safeguard the well-being of our families. We need true fighters, not politicians and certainly not keyboard warriors. We need fearless individuals who understand the value of our freedoms and will fight tooth and nail to preserve them. Together, we can make a difference. We can refuse to be intimidated, standing tall in the face of adversity. We can ensure that America remains the land of opportunity, where our children can thrive and our dreams can flourish. I am Hung Cao, retired Navy Captain, and I am running for the United States Senate. I am committed to being your voice, your champion, and your unwavering fighter. Join me in this fight for our country's future. Together, we can reclaim what we hold dear and ensure that America remains a beacon of hope and liberty for generations to come. I'm Hung Cao and I'm not done fighting for us. Thank you for your support, CAPT Hung Cao, USN (Ret) Former Navy Special Operations Candidate, U.S. Senate (VA) | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | Paid for by Hung Cao for Virginia | | Use of military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense. GOP Presidential 625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314 |

body: ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ Peter, America’s education system has lost sight of its purpose.
